input.main_goal: Reduce the launch plan to one workable mission thread for the next two weeks.
input.current_objective: Define the launch sequence that should anchor the rollout.
input.guidance_next_step: Define the launch sequence that should anchor the rollout.

next_move: should force a directional choice rather than operational busywork

trainerQualityScenarios.title: Sensitive response / communication read

input.situation: A founder needs to respond to an enterprise customer after delaying a promised capability, and must preserve trust without overcommitting the roadmap.
input.main_goal: Protect the account while keeping the roadmap position controlled.
input.current_objective: Prepare the decision explanation for the customer without creating new commitments.
input.guidance_next_step: Prepare the decision explanation for the customer without creating new commitments.

angle: shape a sensitive response with the right tone and position
stay dossier-aware rather than generic writing support
next_move: should produce a usable communication move

rejectIf: sounds like a generic email assistant
fails to protect position while communicating clearly

strongOutputPatterns: Strategy narrows direction, leverage, sequencing, or tradeoffs.
Execution turns the mission into an operational move with clear friction reduction.
Risk identifies a watchpoint and pairs it with verification or protective adjustment.
Communication shapes a dossier-aware message, positioning move, or stakeholder-facing phrasing.
commonFailurePatterns: Different trainers sound too similar in angle or structure.
next_move is vague, abstract, or detached from the trainer role.
Communication drifts into generic writing help.
Risk becomes alarmist instead of measured.
Execution becomes motivational instead of operational.
Strategy stays abstract without narrowing the mission.
